text:
There was once a little old owl who loved gossiping. Her name was Lady Hedwig.

Lady Hedwig hung in her tree all day, listening to every little flutter and screech. By nightfall, she had heard the gossip of every creature beneath the stars.

One night, as she perched contentedly, gossiping about a possum's secret affair with a squirrel, her wise old nose twitched. A peculiar scent floated her way.

"What could that be?" she murmured skeptically.

With cautious curiosity, Lady Hedwig peered down into the tree below. There she saw a pair of tiny rabbits, blissfully snuggled. They would make fine additions to her collection.

Before dawn, Lady Hedwig ambushed the lovers. With precision feathers ruffled, she plucked their fluffy tails away. They squeaked indignantly as she flew off into the twilight.

That evening, under the glow of stars, Lady Hedwig attended a party. From her perch high
